payload.title: Annual Report Narrative 2021
payload.description: Submitted as part of the MTA 2021 Annual Report Pursuant to New York State Public Authorities Law Section 2800(1)

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The logo for the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) is located at the top left. Metropolitan Transportation Authority

payload.description: The MTA carries out these responsibilities directly and through its subsidiaries and affiliates, which are also public benefit corporations.

tool_args.label: MTA New York City Transit
tool_args.raw_text: NYC Transit (Subways and Buses)

payload.description: MTA New York City Transit and its subsidiary MaBSTOA operate all subway transportation and most of the public bus transportation provided within New York City (the Transit System).

tool_args.label: MTA Metro-North Railroad
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad

payload.description: MTA Metro-North Railroad operates commuter rail service between NYC and the northern suburban counties.

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bus Company

payload.description: MTA Bus operates certain bus routes in NYC formerly served by seven private bus operators.

tool_args.label: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

payload.description: MTA Bridges and Tunnels operates all nine of the intra-State toll bridges and tunnels in NYC.

tool_args.label: MTA Long Island Rail Road
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) faced significantly decreased ridership in 2021 as the Covid-19 pandemic continued.

payload.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company
payload.description: MTA Long Island Rail Road operates commuter rail service between NYC and Long Island and within Long Island.

tool_args.label: NYC Department of Transportation
tool_args.raw_text: NYCDOT

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: MTA Construction & Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development (MTA C&D) was officially established in December 2019

payload.description: Responsible for planning, design, and construction of current and future major MTA system expansion projects for the other Related Entities.

tool_args.label: MTA Access-a-Ride
tool_args.raw_text: MTA's Access-a-Ride (AAR), managed by MTA DOB

payload.description: Paratransit service managed by MTA NYCT Department of Buses.

tool_args.label: MTA Police Department
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Police Department (MTAPD), which has a workforce of over 1100 officers and civilian employees

payload.description: Provides systemwide policing through MTA Police Department with jurisdiction over LIRR, Metro-North, and SIR extending across 14 counties in two states.

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2
tool_args.raw_text: Second Avenue Subway, Phase 2 - Committed $221 million to the Second Avenue Subway, Phase 2, as of December 2021. Initial funding for this project is $1.735 billion... total Phase 2 budget to $6.29 billion.

payload.description: Phase 2 of the Second Avenue Subway. Committed $221 million as of December 2021. Initial funding $1.735 billion. FTA approved entry into Engineering phase on January 6, 2022.

tool_args.label: East Side Access
tool_args.raw_text: Committed $10.2 billion to East Side Access (ESA), as of December 2021. This commitment comes out of a current project budget of $11.133 billion...

payload.description: Project bringing LIRR into Grand Central Terminal. Committed $10.2 billion as of December 2021. Current project budget $11.133 billion plus rolling stock reserve of $463 million. Public revenue service date forecast for December 2022.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access
tool_args.raw_text: Penn Station Access Project, which will bring Metro-North into Penn Station and construct four new rail stations in the Bronx... As of December 2021, $198.1 million has been committed...

payload.description: Project to bring Metro-North into Penn Station and construct four new rail stations in the Bronx. Design Build and PMC contracts awarded on December 29, 2021. Total project budget $12.867 billion.

tool_args.label: LIRR Expansion Project
tool_args.raw_text: The design-build contract is to construct approximately 10 miles of third track on the Main Line; eliminate eight street- level grade crossings and provide grade-separated vehicular and pedestrian crossings at six locations.

payload.description: Design-build contract to construct approximately 10 miles of third track on the Main Line; eliminate eight street-level grade crossings and provide grade-separated crossings at six locations. Total budget $2.589 billion. Project completion forecast for mid-2023.

tool_args.label: Central Business District Tolling Program
tool_args.raw_text: Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP), the first of its kind in the United States, is designed to reduce traffic congestion, improve air quality, promote mass transit usage, and raise revenue for key capital projects.

payload.description: First of its kind in the U.S., designed to reduce traffic congestion, improve air quality, promote mass transit usage, and raise revenue for key capital projects. Managed by MTA Bridges and Tunnels. Preliminary design of tolling infrastructure advanced in 2021.

tool_args.label: Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: Advanced the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign, with the final plan approved by the MTA Board in December 2021.

payload.description: The Bronx plan was approved by the MTA Board in December 2021. Borough-wide implementation expected in summer of 2022. Part of the largest overhaul of New York's bus system since the 1950s.

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Weekday Terminal On-Time Performance 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Weekday Terminal On-Time Performance – Subways 88.6% 85.2% -3.4%

payload.description: Weekday Terminal On-Time Performance – Subways 2021 Actual

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Mean Distance Between Failures 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Mean Distance Between Failures – Subways (miles) 146,297 150,363 +2.8%

payload.description: Mean Distance Between Failures – Subways 2021 Actual

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Total Ridership 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Total Ridership – Subways 639,536,812 759,810,246 ** +18.8

payload.description: Total Ridership – Subways 2021 Actual (excluding SIR)

tool_args.label: LIRR On-Time Performance 2021
tool_args.raw_text: On-Time Performance 94.0% 96.3% +2.3%

payload.description: LIRR On-Time Performance 2021 Actual - best since modern record-keeping began in the 1970s
LIRR Total Ridership 2021 Actual

tool_args.label: LIRR Mean Distance Between Failures 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Mean Distance Between Failures (miles) 170,000 231,337 +36.1%

payload.description: LIRR MDBF 2021 Actual - 36.1% above target of 170,000 miles

tool_args.label: Metro-North On-Time Performance 2021
tool_args.raw_text: On-Time Performance (East of Hudson) 94.0 97.1 +3.1

payload.description: Metro-North systemwide OTP for 2021 was above goal at 97.1%

tool_args.label: Metro-North Total Ridership 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Total Ridership (includes connecting services) 23,428,000 30,722,008 +31.1%

payload.description: Metro-North systemwide ridership (includes connecting services note)

tool_args.label: Metro-North Mean Distance Between Failures 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Mean Distance Between Failures (miles) 175,000 190,518 +8.8%

payload.description: Metro-North MDBF 2021 Actual - 8.8% above target
MTA Bus Company Total Ridership 2021 - 55.5% increase

tool_args.label: Bridges and Tunnels Paid Tolled Traffic 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Paid Tolled Traffic 218.6 million 307.3 million +40.6%

payload.description: Paid Tolled Traffic 2021 Actual - 21.4% increase over 2020

tool_args.label: Combined Bus Operations Ridership 2021
tool_args.raw_text: The actual combined ridership for all bus operations ticked up by 6.6 percent from 359.3 million in 2020 to about 383.1 million in 2021.

payload.scope: All MTA Bus Operations (NYCT DOB + MTA Bus)
payload.description: Combined ridership for all bus operations increased 6.6% from 359.3 million in 2020

tool_args.label: Full 24-hour Subway Service Resumed
tool_args.raw_text: on May 17, full 24-hour subway service resumed, ending the first scheduled overnight shutdown in the system's 117-year history.

payload.description: Full 24-hour subway service resumed, ending the first scheduled overnight shutdown in the system's 117-year history.

tool_args.label: MTA Board Approved Bronx Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: Advanced the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign, with the final plan approved by the MTA Board in December 2021.

payload.description: MTA Board approved the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign final plan.

tool_args.label: OMNY Surpassed 100 Million Taps
tool_args.raw_text: In July 2021, OMNY surpassed 100 million 'taps.'

payload.description: OMNY contactless fare payment system surpassed 100 million customer taps.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access Design-Build Contract Awarded
tool_args.raw_text: Design Build and Project Management Consultant (PMC) contracts were both awarded on December 29, 2021.

payload.description: Design Build and Project Management Consultant contracts for Penn Station Access were awarded.

tool_args.label: Janno Lieber Named Acting Board Chair and CEO
tool_args.raw_text: Effective July 31, 2021, Janno Lieber was named Acting Board Chair and CEO of MTA.

payload.description: Janno Lieber was named Acting Board Chair and CEO of MTA after Chairman and CEO Patrick J. Foye resigned on July 29, 2021.

tool_args.label: MTA Employee Vaccine Mandate
tool_args.raw_text: a September 2021 mandate, still in effect, requiring all returning MTA employees to show proof of vaccination or undergo weekly testing.

payload.description: MTA issued a mandate requiring all returning MTA employees to show proof of vaccination or undergo weekly testing.

tool_args.label: Federal Covid-19 Relief to MTA
tool_args.raw_text: The 'CARES' Act in March 2020 provided the MTA with just over $4.0 billion in aid. A second appropriation... an additional $4.0 billion. Finally, the American Rescue Plan Act... the MTA will receive around $6.5 billion.

payload.claim_text: Federal funds from three separate Covid-19 relief packages helped to stabilize the situation during 2021. CARES Act provided $4.0 billion, second appropriation $4.0 billion, American Rescue Plan Act $6.5 billion to MTA.
payload.subject: MTA
payload.description: MTA received approximately $14.5 billion in federal Covid-19 relief across three packages.

tool_args.label: OMNY Rollout Completed
tool_args.raw_text: Supported the continued rollout of One Metro New York (OMNY)... The rollout was completed by January 2021, with 15,000 OMNY readers at all 472 subway stations, readers on all 5,800 buses, and at all Staten Island Railway stations.

payload.claim_text: OMNY rollout completed by January 2021, with 15,000 OMNY readers at all 472 subway stations, readers on all 5,800 buses, and at all Staten Island Railway stations.
payload.subject: OMNY
payload.description: MTA's new contactless fare payment system rollout completed.

tool_args.label: Bridges and Tunnels Support to Mass Transit
tool_args.raw_text: the support Bridges and Tunnels provided to mass transit increased by 65.9 percent in 2021, to $1,377.0 million. The previous year's support was $830.2 million.

payload.claim_text: Bridges and Tunnels provided over $1.37 billion in support to mass transit for 2021, an increase of 65.9% from $830.2 million in 2020.
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
payload.description: Support to mass transit from toll revenue.

tool_args.label: Zero-Emission Bus Fleet by 2040
tool_args.raw_text: Advanced the agency's goal of a zero-emissions bus fleet by 2040. The MTA Board approved the federally funded purchase of 60 low-floor 40-foot all-electric buses in 2021.

payload.claim_text: MTA advanced its goal of a zero-emissions bus fleet by 2040. MTA Board approved the federally funded purchase of 60 low-floor 40-foot all-electric buses in 2021.
payload.subject: MTA Bus Operations
payload.description: Transition to all-electric bus fleet by 2040.

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority Deployment 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Worked with the NYCDOT to expand the number of intersections with Transit Signal Priority (TSP)... The program added TSP at 626 city intersections in 2021 for a current total of 2,156 TSP-enabled intersections. By the end of the year, TSP was deployed to all eligible buses in the MTA fleet.

payload.claim_text: TSP added at 626 city intersections in 2021 for a current total of 2,156 TSP-enabled intersections. TSP deployed to all eligible buses in the MTA fleet.
payload.subject: MTA Bus Operations with NYCDOT
payload.description: Transit Signal Priority expansion in 2021.

tool_args.label: 17 Miles of New Bus Lanes in 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Performance was positively impacted by NYCDOT's addition of 17 miles of new bus lanes and busways in 2021, bringing the total to 142 miles of bus lanes in the MTA network.

payload.claim_text: NYCDOT added 17 new miles of bus lanes and busways in all five boroughs in 2021, bringing total to 142 miles of bus lanes in the MTA network.
payload.subject: NYCDOT with MTA
payload.description: Addition of dedicated bus lanes and busways across the city.

tool_args.label: East Side Access implemented by MTA C&D
tool_args.raw_text: All external capital construction projects for the agencies are now handled by MTA Construction & Development (MTA C&D)... The agency advanced an aggressive schedule of projects related to the LIRR expansion.

payload.description: East Side Access managed by MTA Construction & Development.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access implemented by MTA C&D
tool_args.raw_text: Penn Station Access project, an MTA megaproject managed by MTA Construction & Development.

payload.description: Penn Station Access is an MTA megaproject managed by MTA Construction & Development.

tool_args.label: CBDTP managed by MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: Includes Central Business District Tolling, managed and delivered by Bridges and Tunnels.

payload.description: Central Business District Tolling Program managed and delivered by MTA Bridges and Tunnels.

tool_args.label: Bronx Bus Network Redesign managed by NYCT DOB
tool_args.raw_text: Advanced the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign, with the final plan approved by the MTA Board in December 2021.

payload.description: Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign advanced by MTA Bus Operations.

tool_args.label: Bridges and Tunnels provides financial support to mass transit
tool_args.raw_text: Provided $1.38 billion in total support for the MTA's regional transit operations in 2021... enabling the agency to provide over $1.37 billion in support to mass transit for the year.

payload.description: Bridges and Tunnels provided $1.38 billion in total support for the MTA's regional transit operations in 2021.

tool_args.label: 17 Miles New Bus Lanes and Busways (2021)
tool_args.raw_text: NYCDOT implemented 17 new miles of bus lanes and busways in all five boroughs.

payload.description: NYCDOT implemented 17 new miles of bus lanes and busways in all five boroughs in 2021, bringing total to 142 miles of bus lanes in the MTA network.

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority at 626 Intersections (2021)
tool_args.raw_text: The program added TSP at 626 city intersections in 2021 for a current total of 2,156 TSP-enabled intersections. By the end of the year, TSP was deployed to all eligible buses in the MTA fleet.

payload.description: TSP added at 626 city intersections in 2021 for a total of 2,156 TSP-enabled intersections. Deployed to all eligible buses in the MTA fleet by year end.

tool_args.label: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) System
tool_args.raw_text: The agency continued utilizing the Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) system, which reports bus-lane violations using bus-mounted cameras, triggering fines from $50 to $250. The ABLE system operates on 123 buses serving seven routes. By the end of 2021, the system had captured over 123,500 violations.

payload.description: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement system reports bus-lane violations using bus-mounted cameras. Operates on 123 buses serving seven routes. By end of 2021 captured over 123,500 violations. Fines from $50 to $250.

tool_args.label: Performance Targets Substituted Due to Pandemic
tool_args.raw_text: Due to the anomalies of performance during the Covid-19 pandemic, NYCT Subways did not publish 2021 target goals. Instead, 2020 Actual Metrics are being substituted in this column for purposes of comparison only.

payload.gap_text: Due to the anomalies of performance during the Covid-19 pandemic, NYCT Subways did not publish 2021 target goals. Instead, 2020 Actual Metrics are being substituted in this column for purposes of comparison only.
payload.description: Performance targets for 2021 were not published due to pandemic anomalies; 2020 actuals used as comparison substitute.

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Weekday Major Incidents 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Weekday Major Incidents – Subways (monthly average) 24.3* 33.5 +37.9

payload.description: Weekday Major Incidents – Subways 2021 Actual (monthly average)

tool_args.label: NYCT Subway Total Ridership Including SIR 2021
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT Subways operates SIR but does not include SIR in ridership totals. Subway 2021 ridership including SIR is 761,142,069.

payload.description: NYCT Subway 2021 ridership including Staten Island Railway

tool_args.label: NYCT Bus Speeds 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Bus Speeds NYCT & MTA (avg. route speed, end-to-end) N/A 8.2 N/A

payload.description: Bus Speeds NYCT & MTA (avg. route speed, end-to-end) 2021

tool_args.raw_text: Collisions with Injury Rate (per million vehicles) 0.80 0.67 +16.3%

payload.description: Bridges and Tunnels Collisions with Injury Rate per million vehicles 2021

tool_args.label: LIRR Elevator Availability 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Elevator Availability 98.0% 98.7% +0.7%

payload.description: LIRR Elevator Availability 2021 Actual

tool_args.label: MTA entity parent-subsidiary relationship
tool_args.raw_text: MTA and its subsidiaries, are listed by their legal names... MTA carries out these responsibilities directly and through its subsidiaries and affiliates, which are also public benefit corporations.

payload.description: The Long Island Rail Road Company is a subsidiary of MTA carrying out commuter rail operations.

tool_args.raw_text: MTA and its subsidiaries, are listed by their legal names and estimated number of employees...

payload.description: The Long Island Rail Road Company is a subsidiary of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority.

tool_args.raw_text: MTA and its subsidiaries, are listed by their legal names...

tool_args.raw_text: MTA and its subsidiaries, are listed by their legal names...

tool_args.raw_text: The following entities, listed by their legal names, are affiliates of MTA: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority

payload.description: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (MTA Bridges and Tunnels) is an affiliate of MTA.
